Package org.eventb.core.seqprover.tests

Class TestLib

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.tests.TestLib
public class TestLib extends Object
This is a collection of static methods for conveniently constructing objects used for testing using their string counterparts.

  • Method Details

    • genSeq

      public static IProverSequent genSeq(String sequentAsString, FormulaFactory factory)
      Constructs a simple sequent (only with selected hypotheses and a goal) from a string of the form "shyp1 ;; shyp2 ;; .. ;; shypn |- goal"

      The type environment of the sequent should be inferrable from the predicates in the order in which they appear (eg. "x+1=y ;; x=y |- x/=0" is fine, but "x=y ;; x+1=y |- x/=0" is not since "x=y" cannot be typechecked alone)

      This method is used to easily construct sequents for test cases.
      Parameters:
      sequentAsString - The sequent as a string
      Returns:
      The resulting sequent
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - in case the sequent could not be constructed due to a parsing or typechecking error.

    • genSeq

      public static IProverSequent genSeq(String sequentAsString)

    • genFullSeq

      public static IProverSequent genFullSeq(String sequentAsString)

    • genFullSeq

      public static IProverSequent genFullSeq(String sequentStr, FormulaFactory factory)
      Constructs a sequent from a string of the form " Hyps ;H; Hyps ;S; Hyps |- goal" where the hypothesis list Hyps is of the form " hyp ;; hyp ;; ... ;; hyp ".

      The first list of hypotheses is the list of all hypotheses. The second list (after ;H;) is the list of hidden hypotheses. The third list (after ;S;) is the list of selected hypotheses. The order of hypotheses in the list are kept. Ignoring order, the set of hidden hypotheses and selected hypotheses should be disjoint; if some of their elements are not present in the list of all hypotheses, they will be added to it.

      The type environment of the sequent should be inferrable from the predicates in the order in which they appear in the global set of hypotheses (e.g. "x+1=y ;; x=y " is fine, but "x=y ;; x+1=y " is not since "x=y" cannot be type-checked alone)

      This method is used to easily construct sequents for test cases.
      Parameters:
      sequentAsString - the sequent as a string
      Returns:
      the resulting sequent
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - in case the sequent could not be constructed due to a parsing or type-checking error.

    • genFullSeq

      public static IProverSequent genFullSeq(String sequentAsString, ITypeEnvironmentBuilder typenv)

    • genProofTreeNode

      public static IProofTreeNode genProofTreeNode(String str)

    • genFullSeq

      public static IProverSequent genFullSeq(ITypeEnvironment typenv, String hiddenHypsImage, String defaultHypsImage, String selHypsImage, String goalImage)
      Builds a sequent from several strings detailing its type environment, hypotheses and goal. Sets of hypotheses are given as one string concatenating all hypotheses of the set separated by ";;".

      This method is used to easily construct sequents for test cases.

      Parameters:
      typenv - type environment
      hiddenHypsImage - hidden hypothesis
      defaultHypsImage - visible but not selected hypotheses
      selHypsImage - selected hypotheses
      goalImage - goal
      Returns:
      the sequent build from the given parameters

    • genFullSeq

      public static IProverSequent genFullSeq(String typeEnvImage, String hiddenHypsImage, String defaultHypsImage, String selHypsImage, String goalImage)

    • genFullSeq

      public static IProverSequent genFullSeq(String typeEnvImage, String hiddenHypsImage, String defaultHypsImage, String selHypsImage, String goalImage, FormulaFactory factory)
      Builds a sequent from several strings detailing its type environment, hypotheses and goal. Sets of hypotheses are given as one string concatenating all hypotheses of the set separated by ";;".

      This method is used to easily construct sequents for test cases.

      Parameters:
      typeEnvImage - type environment in string form, should look like "x=S,y=ℙ(ℤ)"
      hiddenHypsImage - hidden hypothesis
      defaultHypsImage - visible but not selected hypotheses
      selHypsImage - selected hypotheses
      goalImage - goal
      Returns:
      the sequent build from the given parameters

    • genFullSeq

      public static IProverSequent genFullSeq(ITypeEnvironment typenv, Set<Predicate> hiddenHyps, Set<Predicate> defaultHyps, Set<Predicate> selectedHyps, Predicate goal)
      Creates a new proof sequent.
      Parameters:
      typenv - type environment
      hiddenHyps - hidden hypotheses
      defaultHyps - visible hypotheses that are not selected
      selectedHyps - selected hypotheses
      goal - goal
      Returns:
      a new proof sequent

    • genSeq

      public static IProverSequent genSeq(FormulaFactory factory, Predicate... predicates)
      Creates a new proof sequent. The type environment is inferred from the given predicates.
      Parameters:
      factory - formula factory used to create the predicates
      predicates - selected hypotheses and goal in this order
      Returns:
      a new proof sequent

    • genSeq

      public static IProverSequent genSeq(Predicate... predicates)
      Creates a new proof sequent. The type environment is inferred from the given predicates. It is assumed that the given predicates do not use any mathematical extension.
      Parameters:
      predicates - selected hypotheses and goal in this order
      Returns:
      a new proof sequent

    • mTypeEnvironment

      public static ITypeEnvironmentBuilder mTypeEnvironment()

    • mTypeEnvironment

      public static ITypeEnvironmentBuilder mTypeEnvironment(String typeEnvImage)

    • mTypeEnvironment

      public static ITypeEnvironmentBuilder mTypeEnvironment(String typeEnvImage, FormulaFactory factory)
      Generates the type environment specified by the given string. The string contains pairs of form ident=type separated by semicolons.

      Example of valid parameters are:

      • ""
      • "x=S"
      • "x=S; y=T"
      • "x=S; r=S↔S"

      Parameters:
      typeEnvImage - image of the type environment to generate
      factory - the formula factory to use for building the result
      Returns:
      the type environment described by the given string

    • parsePred

      public static Predicate parsePred(String str)
      Generates a predicate from a string without type-checking it. The string is parsed with the default formula factory.
      Parameters:
      str - the string version of the predicate
      Returns:
      the parsed predicate

    • parsePred

      public static Predicate parsePred(String str, FormulaFactory factory)
      Generates a predicate from a string without type-checking it. The string is parsed with the given formula factory.
      Parameters:
      str - the string version of the predicate
      factory - the formula factory to use for parsing the string
      Returns:
      the parsed predicate

    • genPred

      public static Predicate genPred(String str)
      Generates a type checked predicate from a string. The string is parsed with the default formula factory. The type environment must be completely inferrable from the given predicate. (e.g., "x=x" will not work since the type of x is unknown)
      Parameters:
      str - the string version of the predicate
      Returns:
      the type checked predicate

    • genPred

      public static Predicate genPred(String str, FormulaFactory factory)
      Generates a type checked predicate from a string. The string is parsed with the given formula factory. The type environment must be completely inferrable from the given predicate. (e.g., "x=x" will not work since the type of x is unknown)
      Parameters:
      str - the string version of the predicate
      factory - the formula factory to use for parsing the string
      Returns:
      the type checked predicate

    • genPred

      public static Predicate genPred(ITypeEnvironmentBuilder typeEnv, String str)
      Generates a type checked predicate from a string and a type environment. As a side-effect the given type environment gets completed with the types inferred during type check.
      Parameters:
      str - The string version of the predicate
      typeEnv - The type environment to check the predicate with
      Returns:
      the type checked predicate

    • genPred

      public static Predicate genPred(ISealedTypeEnvironment typeEnv, String str)
      Generates a type checked predicate from a string and a type environment. This version does not modify the given type environment.
      Parameters:
      str - The string version of the predicate
      typeEnv - The type environment to check the predicate with
      Returns:
      the type checked predicate

      Returns:

      str - the string version of the expression
      factory - formula factory to use for parsing the expression
      Returns:
      the parsed expression

    • genExpr

      public static Expression genExpr(ITypeEnvironmentBuilder typeEnv, String str)
      Generates a type checked expression from a string and a type environment. As a side-effect the given type environment gets completed with the types inferred during type check.
      Parameters:
      str - The string version of the expression
      typeEnv - The type environment to check the expression with
      Returns:
      the type checked expression

    • genExpr

      public static Expression genExpr(ISealedTypeEnvironment typeEnv, String str)
      Generates a type checked expression from a string and a type environment. This version does not modify the given type environment.
      Parameters:
      str - The string version of the expression
      typeEnv - The type environment to check the expression with
      Returns:
      the type checked expression

    • genIdent

      public static FreeIdentifier genIdent(String identImage, String typeImage, FormulaFactory factory)
      Generates a type-checked identifier with the given name and type. All strings are parsed with the formula factory of the given type environment.
      Parameters:
      identImage - the name of the identifier
      typeImage - the type of the identifier in string form
      typenv - some type environment
      Returns:
      the type checked identifier

    • genType

      public static Type genType(String str, FormulaFactory factory)
      Generates a type from a string. The string is parsed with the given factory.
      Parameters:
      str - the string version of the type
      factory - the formula factory to use for parsing
      Returns:
      the type

    • getHypRef

      public static Predicate getHypRef(IProverSequent seq, Predicate hyp)
      Searches the set of hypotheses in the given sequent for the given hypotheses and returns the hypothesis found as it occurs in the sequent. This is useful for extracting the reference of a hypothesis inside a sequent in the case where this is needed.
      Parameters:
      seq - The sequent in whose hyoptheses to search
      hyp - The hypothesis to search for
      Returns:
      The sequent copy of the hypothesis in case it is found, or null otherwise.

    • getFirstHyp

      public static Predicate getFirstHyp(IProverSequent seq)
      Returns the first hypothesis in the given sequent as returned by the iterator in IProverSequent.hypIterable().

      This is useful in test cases where generating the hypothesis from a string is difficult because the typing information needed to typecheck it is present in the sequent, but cannot be inferred from the predicate itself.

      It is recommended to use this method only for sequents with exactly one hypothesis since the order of the hypotheses does not matter and this would lead to more resilient test case code.

      Parameters:
      seq - The sequent in whose hyoptheses to search
      Returns:
      The reference to the first hypothesis in the sequent.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - in case the sequent has no hypotheses

    • assertNoProblem

      public static void assertNoProblem(IResult result, Object object, String message)
